name: bluetooth pin
detail: Configure a new Bluetooth pin.
syntax: bluetooth pin pin

params.name: pin
params.description: Pairing pin for the Bluetooth interface. The pin is a 4-digit number.

usageSummary: The bluetooth pin command can be configured either in the interface configuration or global configuration mode.  Cisco recommends using the global configuration mode to configure the Bluetooth pin.

name: carrier-delay
detail: Modify the default carrier delay time on a main physical interface.
syntax: carrier-delay { seconds | msec milliseconds }

params.name: seconds
params.description: Specifies the carrier transition delay, in seconds. The range is from 0 to 60. The default is 2. msec milliseconds Specifies the carrier transition delay, in seconds. The range is from 0 to 1000.

usageSummary: If a link goes down and comes back before the carrier delay timer expires, the down state is effectively filtered, and the rest of the software on the router is not aware that a link-down event has occurred.  Therefore, a large carrier delay timer results in fewer link-up/link-down events being detected.  However, setting the carrier delay time to 0 means that every link-up/link-down event is detected.

name: debug ilpower
detail: Enable debugging of the power controller and Power over Ethernet (PoE) system.
syntax: debug ilpower {cdp | event | ha | ipc | police | port | powerman | registries | scp | sense}
noForm: no debug ilpower {cdp | event | ha | ipc | police | port | powerman | registries | scp | sense}

params.name: cdp
params.description: Displays PoE Cisco Discovery Protocol (CDP) debug messages.

params.name: event
params.description: Displays PoE event debug messages.

params.name: ha
params.description: Displays PoE high-availability messages.

params.name: ipc
params.description: Displays PoE Inter-Process Communication (IPC) debug messages.

params.name: police
params.description: Displays PoE police debug messages.

params.name: port
params.description: Displays PoE port manager debug messages. powerman Displays PoE power management debug messages. registries Displays PoE registries debug messages.

params.name: scp
params.description: Displays PoE SCP debug messages.

params.name: sense
params.description: Displays PoE sense debug messages.

usageSummary: This command is supported only on PoE-capable switches.  When you enable debugging on a switch stack, it is enabled only on the active switch.  To enable debugging on a stack member, you can start a session from the active switch by using the session switch-number EXEC command.

name: debug interface
detail: Enable debugging of interface-related activities.
syntax: debug interface {interface-id | counters {exceptions | protocol memory} | null interface-number | port-channel port-channel-number | states|vlan vlan-id} | port-channel port-channel-number | states|vlan vlan-id}
noForm: no debug interface {interface-id | counters {exceptions | protocol memory} | null interface-number

params.name: interface-id
params.description: ID of the physical interface. Displays debug messages for the specified physical port, identified by type switch number/module number/port, for example, gigabitethernet 1/0/2.

params.name: null interface-number
params.description: Displays debug messages for null interfaces. The interface number is always 0.

params.name: port-channel
params.description: Displays debug messages for the specified EtherChannel port-channel

params.name: port-channel-number
params.description: interface. The port-channel-number range is 1 to 48.

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: Displays debug messages for the specified VLAN. The vlan range is 1 to 4094.

params.name: counters
params.description: Displays counters debugging information.

params.name: exceptions
params.description: Displays debug messages when a recoverable exceptional condition occurs during the computation of the interface packet and data rate statistics.

params.name: protocol memory
params.description: Displays debug messages for memory operations of protocol counters.

params.name: states
params.description: Displays intermediary debug messages when an interface's state transitions.

usageSummary: If you do not specify a keyword, all debug messages appear.  The undebug interface command is the same as the no debug interface command.  When you enable debugging on a switch stack, it is enabled only on the active switch.

name: debug lldp packets
detail: Enable debugging of Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) packets.
syntax: debug lldp packets

params.name: This
params.description: commnd has no arguments or keywords.

usageSummary: The undebug lldp packets command is the same as the no debug lldp packets command.  When you enable debugging on a switch stack, it is enabled only on the active switch.  To enable debugging on a stack member, you can start a session from the active switch by using the session switch-number EXEC command.

name: debug platform poe
detail: Enable debugging of a Power over Ethernet (PoE) port.
syntax: debug platform poe [error | info] [switch switch-number]
noForm: no debug platform poe [error | info] [switch switch-number]

params.name: error
params.description: (Optional) Displays PoE-related error debug messages.

params.name: info
params.description: (Optional) Displays PoE-related information debug messages. switch switch-number (Optional) Specifies the stack member. This keyword is supported only on stacking-capable switches.

usageSummary: The undebug platform poe command is the same as the no debug platform poe command.  Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 15.

name: duplex
detail: Specify the duplex mode of operation for a port.
syntax: duplex {auto | full | half}
usageSummary: For Gigabit Ethernet ports, setting the port to auto has the same effect as specifying full if the attached device does not autonegotiate the duplex parameter.

name: enable
detail: Enable the 100 GigabitEthernet interface.
syntax: enable
usageSummary: Use the enable command in the interface configuration mode, to enable the 100 GigabitEthernet interface.  Use the no version of the command to disable the 100 GigabitEthernet interface.  To display the current state of an interface, enter the show interface interface-id command in privileged EXEC mode.

name: errdisable detect cause
detail: Enable error-disable detection for a specific cause or for all causes.
syntax: errdisable detect cause {all | arp-inspection | bpduguard shutdown vlan | dhcp-rate-limit | dtp-flap | gbic-invalid | inline-power | link-flap | loopback | pagp-flap | pppoe-ia-rate-limit | psp shutdown vlan | security-violation shutdown vlan | sfp-config-mismatch} dtp-flap | gbic-invalid | inline-power | link-flap | loopback | pagp-flap | pppoe-ia-rate-limit | psp shutdown vlan | security-violation shutdown vlan | sfp-config-mismatch}
noForm: no errdisable detect cause {all | arp-inspection | bpduguard shutdown vlan | dhcp-rate-limit |

params.name: all
params.description: Enables error detection for all error-disabled causes.

params.name: arp-inspection
params.description: Enables error detection for dynamic Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) inspection.

params.name: bpduguard shutdown vlan
params.description: Enables per-VLAN error-disable for BPDU guard.

params.name: dhcp-rate-limit
params.description: Enables error detection for DHCP snooping.

params.name: dtp-flap
params.description: Enables error detection for the Dynamic Trunking Protocol (DTP) flapping.

params.name: gbic-invalid
params.description: Enables error detection for an invalid Gigabit Interface Converter (GBIC) module. Note This error refers to an invalid small form-factor pluggable (SFP) module.

params.name: inline-power
params.description: Enables error detection for the Power over Ethernet (PoE) error-disabled cause. Note This keyword is supported only on switches with PoE ports.

params.name: link-flap
params.description: Enables error detection for link-state flapping.

params.name: loopback
params.description: Enables error detection for detected loopbacks.

params.name: pagp-flap
params.description: Enables error detection for the Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) flap error-disabled cause.

params.name: pppoe-ia-rate-limit
params.description: Enables error detection for the PPPoE Intermediate Agent rate-limit error-disabled cause.

params.name: psp shutdown vlan
params.description: Enables error detection for protocol storm protection (PSP).

params.name: security-violation shutdown
params.description: Enables voice aware 802.1x security. vlan Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.15.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 301 Interface and Hardware Components errdisable detect cause

params.name: sfp-config-mismatch
params.description: Enables error detection on an SFP configuration mismatch.

usageSummary: A cause (such as a link-flap or dhcp-rate-limit) is the reason for the error-disabled state.  When a cause is detected on an interface, the interface is placed in an error-disabled state, an operational state that is similar to a link-down state.  When a port is error-disabled, it is effectively shut down, and no traffic is sent or received on the port.

params.name: loopback

params.name: pagp-flap
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP)-flap error-disabled state. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.15.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 305 Interface and Hardware Components errdisable recovery cause

params.name: port-mode-failure
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the port mode change failure error-disabled state.

params.name: pppoe-ia-rate-limit
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the PPPoE IA rate limit error-disabled state.

params.name: psecure-violation

params.name: psp

params.name: security-violation

params.name: sfp-config-mismatch

params.name: storm-control
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from a storm control error.

params.name: udld
params.description: Enables the timer to recover from the UniDirectional Link Detection (UDLD) error-disabled state.

usageSummary: A cause (such as all or BDPU guard) is defined as the reason that the error-disabled state occurred.  When a cause is detected on an interface, the interface is placed in the error-disabled state, an operational state similar to link-down state.  When a port is error-disabled, it is effectively shut down, and no traffic is sent or received on the port.

name: hw-module beacon
detail: Control the beacon LED on a device.
syntax: Note This command description is not applicable to the C9500-32C, C9500-32QC, C9500-48Y4C, and C9500-24Y4C models of the Cisco Catalyst 9500 Series Switches. Cisco IOS XE Fuji 16.8.x and Earlier Releases hw-module beacon { off | on } switch switch-number Cisco IOS XE Fuji 16.9.1 and Later Releases hw-module beacon slot { switch-number | active | standby } { off | on }

params.name: off
params.description: Turns the beacon off.

params.name: on
params.description: Turns the beacon on.

params.name: switch switch-number
params.description: Specifies the switch to be controlled. • switch-number: Switch number. The range is from 1 to 8. slot {switch-number | active | standby} Specifies the switch to be controlled. • switch-number: Switch number. The range is from 1 to 8. • active: Specifies the active switch. • standby: Specifies the standby switch.

modes: Global configuration (config) (Cisco IOS XE Fuji 16.8.x and Earlier Releases) Privileged EXEC (#) (Cisco IOS XE Fuji 16.9.1 and Later Releases)

name: hw-module beacon

params.name: rp {active | standby}
params.description: Specifies the active or the standby Supervisor to be controlled.

params.name: slot slot-number
params.description: Specifies the slot to be controlled.

params.name: ssd
params.description: Specifies the SSD to be controlled.

params.name: off
params.description: Turns the beacon off.

params.name: on
params.description: Turns the beacon on.

params.name: status
params.description: Displays the status of the beacon.

usageSummary: Use the hw-module beacon slot slot-number command to enable or disable the module slot LED and also check its status.  Blue indicates the slot LED is on and black indicates that it is off.  Use the hw-module beacon rp active {off | on} command to enable or disable the active supervisor LED.

name: ip mtu
detail: Set the IP maximum transmission unit (MTU) size of routed packets on all routed ports of the switch or switch stack.
syntax: ip mtu bytes

params.name: bytes
params.description: MTU size, in bytes. The range is from 68 up to the system MTU value (in bytes).

usageSummary: The upper limit of the IP value is based on the switch or switch stack configuration and refers to the currently applied system MTU value.  For more information about setting the MTU sizes, see the system mtu global configuration command.  To return to the default IP MTU setting, you can apply the default ip mtu command or the no ip mtu command on the interface.

name: ipv6 mtu
detail: Set the IPv6 maximum transmission unit (MTU) size of routed packets on all routed ports of the switch or switch stack.
syntax: ipv6 mtu bytes

params.name: bytes
params.description: MTU size, in bytes. The range is from 1280 up to the system MTU value (in bytes).

usageSummary: The upper limit of the IPv6 MTU value is based on the switch or switch stack configuration and refers to the currently applied system MTU value.  For more information about setting the MTU sizes, see the system mtu global configuration command.  To return to the default IPv6 MTU setting, you can apply the default ipv6 mtu command or the no ipv6 mtu command on the interface.

name: lldp
detail: Enable Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) on an interface.
syntax: lldp {med-tlv-select tlv | receive | tlv-select power-management | transmit}
noForm: no lldp {med-tlv-select tlv | receive | tlv-select power-management | transmit}

params.name: med-tlv-select
params.description: Selects an LLDP Media Endpoint Discovery (MED) time-length-value (TLV) element to send.

params.name: tlv
params.description: String that identifies the TLV element. Valid values are the following: • inventory-management— LLDP MED Inventory Management TLV. • location— LLDP MED Location TLV. • network-policy— LLDP MED Network Policy TLV. • power-management— LLDP MED Power Management TLV.

params.name: receive
params.description: Enables the interface to receive LLDP transmissions.

params.name: tlv-select
params.description: Selects the LLDP TLVs to send.

params.name: power-management
params.description: Sends the LLDP Power Management TLV.

params.name: transmit
params.description: Enables LLDP transmission on the interface.

usageSummary: This command is supported on 802. 1 media types.  If the interface is configured as a tunnel port, LLDP is automatically disabled.

name: link debounce timer
detail: To configure debounce timer on a port, use the link debounce [time debounce-time]
syntax: link debounce time debounce_time

params.name: debounce_time
params.description: Sets the debounce time in miliseconds. The range is from 120 to 10000.

name: mode
detail: Configure power stack mode for the power stack.
syntax: mode {power-shared | redundant} [strict]

params.name: power-shared
params.description: Sets the power stack to operate in power-shared mode. This is the default.

params.name: redundant
params.description: Sets the power stack to operate in redundant mode. The largest power supply is removed from the power pool to be used as backup power in case one of the other power supplies fails.

params.name: strict
params.description: (Optional) Configures the power stack mode to run a strict power budget. The stack power needs cannot exceed the available power.

usageSummary: This command is available only on switch stacks running the IP Base or IP Services feature set.  To access power-stack configuration mode, enter the stack-power stack power stack name global configuration command.  Entering the no mode command sets the switch to the defaults of power-shared and non-strict mode.

name: monitoring
detail: Enable monitoring of all optical transceivers and to specify the time period for monitoring the transceivers.
syntax: monitoring [ interval seconds ]

params.name: interval
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the time interval for monitoring optical transceivers. seconds The range is from 300 to 3600 seconds, and the default interval time is 600 seconds.

usageSummary: You need digital optical monitoring (DOM) feature and transceiver module compatibility information to configure the monitoring command.  Refer to the compatibility matrix to get the lists of Cisco platforms and minimum required software versions to support Gigabit Ethernet transceiver modules.  Gigabit Ethernet Transceivers transmit and receive Ethernet frames at a rate of a gigabit per second, as defined by the IEEE 802.

name: network-policy
detail: Apply a network-policy profile to an interface.
syntax: network-policy profile-number

params.name: profile-number
params.description: The network-policy profile number to apply to the interface.

usageSummary: Use the network-policy profile number interface configuration command to apply a profile to an interface.  You cannot apply the switchport voice vlan command on an interface if you first configure a network-policy profile on it.  However, if switchport voice vlan vlan-id is already configured on the interface, you can apply a network-policy profile on the interface.

name: network-policy profile
detail: Create a network-policy profile and to enter network-policy configuration mode.
syntax: network-policy profile profile-number

params.name: profile-number
params.description: Network-policy profile number. The range is 1 to 4294967295.

usageSummary: Use the network-policy profile global configuration command to create a profile and to enter network-policy profile configuration mode.  To return to privileged EXEC mode from the network-policy profile configuration mode, enter the exit command.  When you are in network-policy profile configuration mode, you can create the profile for voice and voice signaling by specifying the values for VLAN, class of service (CoS), differentiated services code point (DSCP), and tagging mode.

name: platform usb disable
detail: Disable all the USB ports on a device.
syntax: platform usb disable
usageSummary: The platform usb disable command disables all the USB ports on both stacked and standalone devices, but not Bluetooth dongles connected to USB ports.  This command also disables all USB ports in ROMMON, which can be reenabled by using the no platform usb disable command in global configuration mode.

name: port-settings
detail: Configure port settings for an interface.
syntax: port-settings { speed { 10 | 100 | 1000 | auto | auto-list } } { duplex { auto | full | half } } { autoneg { disable | enable } } | full | half } } { autoneg { disable | enable } }
noForm: no port-settings port-settings { speed { 10 | 100 | 1000 | auto | auto-list } } { duplex { auto

params.name: speed
params.description: Configures the speed for an interface.
Configures the interface to transmit at 10 Mbps.
Configures the interface to transmit at 100 Mbps.
Configures the interface to transmit at 1000 Mbps.

params.name: auto
params.description: Enables auto speed configuration. This keyword autonegotiates speed with the connected device.

params.name: auto-list
params.description: Advertises a subset of speeds for auto-negotiation. Enter the auto-list keyword with a specific speed to enable the interface to autonegotiate only at the specified speed.

params.name: duplex
params.description: Configures duplex operation on an interface.

params.name: auto
params.description: Enable auto-duplex configuration.

params.name: full
params.description: Specifies full-duplex operation.

params.name: half
params.description: Specifies half-duplex operation.

params.name: autoneg
params.description: Enables autonegotiation. The interface automatically operates at half-duplex or full-duplex mode depending on environmental factors, such as the type of media and the transmission speeds for the peer routers, hubs, and switches used in the network configuration. The autoneg keyword also enables autonegociation on speed configuration and flow-control.

params.name: disable
params.description: Disables auto negotiation.

params.name: enable
params.description: Enables autonegotiation.

modes: Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.15.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 325 Interface and Hardware Components port-settings
usageSummary: Use the port-settings command to simultaneously or separately configure the speed, duplex, and auto negotiation for an interface.  This command can configure port settings for an interface, an interface range, or a port-channel interface.  When using a single command to configure multiple parameters of the port-settings command, the order must be, speed, duplex, and autoneg.

name: power-priority
detail: Configure Cisco StackPower power-priority values for a switch in a power stack and for its high-priority and low-priority PoE ports.
syntax: power-priority {high value | low value | switch value}

params.name: high value
params.description: Sets the power priority for the ports configured as high-priority ports. The range is 1 to 27, with 1 as the highest priority. The high value must be lower than the value set for the low-priority ports and higher than the value set for the switch.

params.name: low value
params.description: Sets the power priority for the ports configured as low-priority ports. The range is 1 to 27. The low value must be higher than the value set for the high-priority ports and the value set for the switch.

params.name: switch
params.description: Sets the power priority for the switch. The range is 1 to 27. The switch value must be lower

params.name: value
params.description: than the values set for the low and high-priority ports.

usageSummary: To access switch stack-power configuration mode, enter the stack-power switch switch-number global configuration command.  Cisco StackPower power-priority values determine the order for shutting down switches and ports when power is lost and load shedding must occur.  Priority values are from 1 to 27; the highest numbers are shut down first.

name: power supply
detail: Configure and manage the internal power supplies on a switch.
syntax: power supply stack-member-number slot {A | B} {off | on}

params.name: stack-member-number
params.description: Stack member number for which to configure the internal power supplies. The range is 1 to 9, depending on the number of switches in the stack. This parameter is available only on stacking-capable switches.

params.name: slot
params.description: Selects the switch power supply to set.

params.name: A
params.description: Selects the power supply in slot A.

params.name: B
params.description: Selects the power supply in slot B. Note Power supply slot B is the closest slot to the outer edge of the switch.

params.name: off
params.description: Sets the switch power supply to off.

params.name: on
params.description: Sets the switch power supply to on.

usageSummary: The power supply command applies to a switch or to a switch stack where all switches are the same platform. name: show ip interface
detail: Display the usability status of interfaces configured for IP.
syntax: show ip interface [type number] [brief]

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional) Interface type.

params.name: number
params.description: (Optional) Interface number.

params.name: brief
params.description: (Optional) Displays a summary of the usability status information for each interface. Note The output of the show ip interface brief command displays information of all the available interfaces whether or not the corresponding network module for these interfaces are connected. These interfaces can be configured if the network module is connected. Run the show interface status command to see which network modules are connected. This is not applicable for Cisco Catalyst 9500 Series High-Performance switches.

usageSummary: The Cisco IOS software automatically enters a directly connected route in the routing table if the interface is usable (which means that it can send and receive packets).  If an interface is not usable, the directly connected routing entry is removed from the routing table.  Removing the entry lets the software use dynamic routing protocols to determine backup routes to the network, if any.

name: show interfaces
detail: Display the administrative and operational status of all interfaces or for a specified interface.
syntax: show interfaces [ interface-id | vlan vlan-id ] [ accounting | capabilities [ module number ] | description | etherchannel | flowcontrol | link [ module number ] | private-vlan mapping | pruning | stats | status [ err-disabled | inactive ] | trunk ]

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) ID of the interface. Valid interfaces include physical ports (including type, stack member for stacking-capable switches, module, and port number) and port channels. The port channel range is 1 to 128. Note This command does not report output drops for Catalyst 9500X Series Switches.

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) VLAN identification. The range is 1 to 4094.

params.name: accounting
params.description: (Optional) Displays accounting information on the interface, including active protocols and input and output packets and octets. Note The display shows only packets processed in software; hardware-switched packets do not appear.

params.name: capabilities
params.description: (Optional) Displays the capabilities of all interfaces or the specified interface, including the features and options that you can configure on the interface. Though visible in the command line help, this option is not available for VLAN IDs.

params.name: module number
params.description: (Optional) Displays capabilities of all interfaces on the switch or specified stack member. The range is 1 to 9. This option is not available if you entered a specific interface ID.

params.name: description
params.description: (Optional) Displays the administrative status and description set for interfaces. Note The output of the show interfaces description command displays information of all the available interfaces whether or not the corresponding network module for these interfaces are connected. These interfaces can be configured if the network module is connected. Run the show interface status command to see which network modules are connected. This is not applicable for Cisco Catalyst 9500 Series High-Performance switches. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.15.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 348 Interface and Hardware Components show interfaces

params.name: etherchannel
params.description: (Optional) Displays interface EtherChannel information.

params.name: flowcontrol
params.description: (Optional) Displays interface flow control information.

params.name: link [modulenumber]
params.description: (Optional) Displays the up time and down time of the interface.

params.name: private-vlan mapping
params.description: (Optional) Displays private-VLAN mapping information for the VLAN switch virtual interfaces (SVIs). This keyword is not available if the switch is running the LAN base feature set.

params.name: pruning
params.description: (Optional) Displays trunk VTP pruning information for the interface.

params.name: stats
params.description: (Optional) Displays the input and output packets by switching the path for the interface.

params.name: status
params.description: (Optional) Displays the status of the interface. A status of unsupported in the Type field means that a non-Cisco small form-factor pluggable (SFP) module is inserted in the module slot.

params.name: err-disabled
params.description: (Optional) Displays interfaces in an error-disabled state.

params.name: inactive
params.description: (Optional) Displays interfaces in an inactive state.

params.name: trunk
params.description: (Optional) Displays interface trunk information. If you do not specify an interface, only information for active trunking ports appears.

params.name: Note
params.description: Though visible in the command-line help strings, the crb, fair-queue, irb, mac-accounting, precedence, random-detect, rate-limit, and shape keywords are not supported.

usageSummary: The show interfaces capabilities command with different keywords has these results: • Use the show interface capabilities module number command to display the capabilities of all interfaces on that switch in the stack.  If there is no switch with that module number in the stack, there is no output.  • Use the show interfaces interface-id capabilities to display the capabilities of the specified interface.

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) ID of the interface. Valid interfaces include physical ports (including type, stack member for stacking-capable switches, module, and port number) and port channels. The port channel range is 1 to 48. module number (Optional) Displays switchport configuration of all interfaces on the switch or specified stack member. The range is 1 to 9. params.name: interface-id

name: show inventory
detail: Display the product inventory listing of all Cisco products installed in the networking device.
syntax: show inventory {fru | oid | raw} [entity] fru (Optional) Retrieves information about all Field Replaceable Units (FRUs) installed in the Cisco networking device. oid (Optional) Retrieves information about the vendor specific hardware registration identifier referred to as object identifier (OID). The OID identifies the MIB object’s location in the MIB hierarchy, and provides a means of accessing the MIB object in a network of managed devices raw (Optional) Retrieves information about all Cisco products referred to as entities installed in the Cisco networking device, even if the entities do not have a product ID (PID) value, a unique device identifier (UDI), or other physical identification. entity (Optional) Name of a Cisco entity (for example, chassis, backplane, module, or slot). A quoted string may be used to display very specific UDI information; for example “sfslot 1” will display the UDI information for slot 1 of an entity named sfslot.
usageSummary: The show inventory command retrieves and displays inventory information about each Cisco product in the form of a UDI.  The UDI is a combination of three separate data elements: a product identifier (PID), a version identifier (VID), and the serial number (SN).  The PID is the name by which the product can be ordered; it has been historically called the “Product Name” or “Part Number.

Table 23: show platform software process memory Field Descriptions
PID Displays the process ID that is used by the operating system to identify and keep track of the processes.
RSS Displays the Resident Set Size (in kilobytes (KB)) that shows how much memory is allocated to that process in the RAM.
PSS Displays the Proportional Set Size of a process. This is the count of pages it has in memory, where each page is divided by the number of processes sharing it.
Heap Displays where all user-allocated memory is located.
Name Displays the command name associated with the process. Different threads in the same process may have different command values.

name: speed
detail: Specify the speed of a port.
syntax: Note Available configuration options depend on the switch model and transceiver module installed. Options include 10, 100, 1000, 2500, 5000, 10000, 25000, 40000, 100000 speed {10 | 100 | 1000 | 2500 | 5000 | 10000 | 25000 | 40000 | 100000 | auto [10 | 100 | 1000 | 2500 | 5000] | nonegotiate}

params.description: Specifies that the port runs at 10 Mbps.
Specifies that the port runs at 100 Mbps.
Specifies that the port runs at 1000 Mbps. This option is valid and visible only on 10/100/1000 Mb/s ports.
Specifies that the port runs at 2500 Mbps. This option is valid and visible only on multi-Gigabit-supported Ethernet ports.
Specifies that the port runs at 5000 Mbps. This option is valid and visible only on multi-Gigabit-supported Ethernet ports.
Specifies that the port runs at 10000 Mbps operation.
Specifies that the port runs at 25000 Mbps operation.
Specifies that the port runs at 40000 Mbps operation.
Specifies that the port runs at 100000 Mbps operation.

params.name: auto
params.description: Detects the speed at which the port should run, automatically, based on the port at the other end of the link. If you use the 10, 100, 1000, 2500, or 5000 keyword with the auto keyword, the port autonegotiates only at the specified speeds. nonegotiate Disables autonegotiation, and the port runs at 1000 Mbps.

usageSummary: You cannot configure speed on 10-Gigabit Ethernet ports.  Except for the 1000BASE-T small form-factor pluggable (SFP) modules, you can configure the speed to not negotiate (nonegotiate) when an SFP module port is connected to a device that does not support autonegotiation.  The keywords, 2500 and 5000 are visible only on multi-Gigabit (m-Gig) Ethernet supporting devices.

name: switchport block
detail: Prevent unknown multicast or unicast packets from being forwarded.
syntax: switchport block {multicast | unicast}

params.name: unicast
params.description: Specifies that unknown unicast traffic should be blocked.

usageSummary: By default, all traffic with unknown MAC addresses is sent to all ports.  You can block unknown multicast or unicast traffic on protected or nonprotected ports.  If unknown multicast or unicast traffic is not blocked on a protected port, there could be security issues.

name: system debounce link-down
detail: Configure debounce down timer on the Cisco Catalyst 9500X Series Switches.
syntax: system debounce link-down debounce_time

params.name: debounce_time
params.description: Sets the debounce time in miliseconds. The range is from 120 to 10000.

usageSummary: To validate this configuration, use the show run command.

name: system debounce link-up
detail: Configure debounce up timer on the Cisco Catalyst 9500X Series Switches.
syntax: system debounce link-up debounce_time

params.name: debounce_time
params.description: Sets the debounce time in miliseconds. The range is from 120 to 10000.

usageSummary: To validate this configuration, use the show run command.

name: system mtu
detail: Set the global maximum packet size or MTU size for switched packets on Gigabit Ethernet and 10-Gigabit Ethernet ports.
syntax: system mtu bytes
usageSummary: You can verify your setting by entering the show system mtu privileged EXEC command.  The switch does not support the MTU on a per-interface basis.  If you enter a value that is outside the allowed range for the specific type of interface, the value is not accepted.

name: voice-signaling vlan
detail: Create a network-policy profile for the voice-signaling application type.
syntax: voice-signaling vlan {vlan-id [cos cos-value | dscp dscp-value] | dot1p [cos l2-priority | dscp dscp] | none | untagged}

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) The VLAN for voice traffic. The range is 1 to 4094.

params.name: cos cos-value
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the Layer 2 priority class of service (CoS) for the configured VLAN. The range is 0 to 7; the default is 5. dscp dscp-value (Optional) Specifies the differentiated services code point (DSCP) value for the configured VLAN. The range is 0 to 63; the default is 46.

params.name: dot1p
params.description: (Optional) Configures the phone to use IEEE 802.1p priority tagging and to use VLAN 0 (the native VLAN).

params.name: none
params.description: (Optional) Does not instruct the Cisco IP phone about the voice VLAN. The phone uses the configuration from the phone key pad.

params.name: untagged
params.description: (Optional) Configures the phone to send untagged voice traffic. This is the default for the phone.

usageSummary: Use the network-policy profile global configuration command to create a profile and to enter network-policy profile configuration mode.  The voice-signaling application type is for network topologies that require a different policy for voice signaling than for voice media.  This application type should not be advertised if all of the same network policies apply as those advertised in the voice policy TLV.

name: voice vlan
detail: Create a network-policy profile for the voice application type.
syntax: voice vlan {vlan-id [cos cos-value | dscp dscp-value] | dot1p [cos l2-priority | dscp dscp] | none | untagged}

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) The VLAN for voice traffic. The range is 1 to 4094.

params.name: cos cos-value
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the Layer 2 priority class of service (CoS) for the configured VLAN. The range is 0 to 7; the default is 5. dscp dscp-value (Optional) Specifies the differentiated services code point (DSCP) value for the configured VLAN. The range is 0 to 63; the default is 46.

params.name: dot1p
params.description: (Optional) Configures the phone to use IEEE 802.1p priority tagging and to use VLAN 0 (the native VLAN).

params.name: none
params.description: (Optional) Does not instruct the Cisco IP phone about the voice VLAN. The phone uses the configuration from the phone key pad.

params.name: untagged
params.description: (Optional) Configures the phone to send untagged voice traffic. This is the default for the phone.

usageSummary: Use the network-policy profile global configuration command to create a profile and to enter network-policy profile configuration mode.  The voice application type is for dedicated IP telephones and similar devices that support interactive voice services.  These devices are typically deployed on a separate VLAN for ease of deployment and enhanced security through isolation from data applications.
